title: Sigsegv when stopping a container
body:
### Issue Description

Podman crashes when stopping a container in a high concurrency scenario - automated tests starting and stopping hundreds of containers in a timespan of < 15 s. See the stack trace below.

podman version: 5.4.2

stopped 3d59e071545735d973dafcd76cd6baddba5f22a62693a311d6a6d0d87d188029
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0x1590adb]

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*storageService).UnmountContainerImage(0x0, {0xc0008806c0?, 0x1b?}, 0x0)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/storage.go:235 +0x3b
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).unmount(0xc000261220, 0x40?)

        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal.go:2019 +0x4c5
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).cleanup(0xc000261220, {0x20c0c00, 0x2f40760})

        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal.go:1459 +0x5c5
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).StopWithTimeout(0xc000261220?, 0xa?)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_api.go:320 +0xce
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).Stop(...)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_api.go:290
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Runtime).Migrate(0xc0004b8700, {0x0, 0x0})
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/runtime_migrate_linux.go:76 +0x2e5
github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi.(*ContainerEngine).Migrate(0x0?, {0x0?, 0xe53a27cc44d51c3e?}, {{0x0?, 0x0?}})
        github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi/system.go:307 +0x25
github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/system.migrate(0x2e3a000?, {0x2f40760?, 0x0?, 0x0?})
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/system/migrate.go:56 +0x45
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).execute(0x2e3a000, {0xc00003ea40, 0x0, 0x0})
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:989 +0xabb
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteC(0x2e22d20)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1117 +0x44f
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).Execute(...)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1041
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteContext(...)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1034
main.Execute()
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/root.go:119 +0x57
main.main()
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/main.go:62 +0x4b2

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Execute a test that starts and stops containers hundreds of times with some parallelism - several threads starting and stopping containers in parallel.



### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

comments.body: did you script this? can you share?

> Execute a test that starts and stops containers hundreds of times with some parallelism - several threads starting and stopping containers in parallel.

That is incompatible with the stack trace you provided which is clearly from a `podman system migrate` command. Why are you running this command?

At the very least it is never useful to run that concurrently while starting/stopping other containers.

In addition you seem to be using an outdated podman version, upstream only supports the latest version. In particular this stack trace looks exactly like what I already fixed in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27604 which is in podman v5.7.1

> > Execute a test that starts and stops containers hundreds of times with some parallelism - several threads starting and stopping containers in parallel.
> 
> That is incompatible with the stack trace you provided which is clearly from a `podman system migrate` command. Why are you running this command?
> 
> At the very least it is never useful to run that concurrently while starting/stopping other containers.
> 
> In addition you seem to be using an outdated podman version, upstream only supports the latest version. In particular this stack trace looks exactly like what I already fixed in [#27604](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27604) which is in podman v5.7.1

After multiple starts and stops of containers Podman breaks and "podman ps" reports the following error:
`ERRO[0000] invalid internal status, try resetting the pause process with "podman system migrate": could not find any running process: no such process`

Hence the system migrate command.

I reported it against Podman 5.4.2 because that is the version provided by Debian Trixie.
